{"problem_standardized_math":"Let\n\\[\nf_j(x)=\\frac{1}{q^2}\\left[1+\\frac{q^2}{2}\\left((x_j-x_{j+q+1})^2+(x_{j+1}-x_{j+q})^2\\right)\\right]^{1/2}.\n\\]\nCompute the gradient of the element function \(f_j\) with respect to the full vector \(x\). Show that this gradient contains at most four nonzero components, and that two of these four nonzero components are the negatives of the other two. Compute the Hessian of \(f_j\), and show that among its 16 nonzero entries only three distinct magnitudes occur. Also show that this Hessian is singular."}
